title: Chiss civil conflict
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Otheruses|Civil war}} {{Conjecture|legends}} {{Citation}} {{War |prev=[[Third Vagaari War]] |conc= |next= |image= |name=Chiss civil conflict |begin= |end=By [[28 ABY/Legends|28 ABY]]''[[Dark Nest I: The Joiner King]]'' establishes that the dispute was resolved before [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] visited [[Csilla/Legends|Csilla]] during the [[Hunt for Zonama Sekot]] during the events of ''[[The New Jedi Order: Force Heretic II: Refugee]]'', which took place in [[28 ABY/Legends|28 ABY]] per ''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]''. |date= |place=[[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]]''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' |result=Reduction in number of Chiss [[Ruling Families/Legends|Ruling Families]]''[[Dark Nest I: The Joiner King]]'' |battles= |side1=*[[Ruling Families/Legends|Four Families]] **[[Csapla family]] **[[Nuruodo family]] **[[Inrokini family]] **[[Sabosen family]] *[[Syndic Mitth'raw'nuruodo's Household Phalanx]] |side2=*Three [[Killik/Legends|Killik]]-allied Ruling Families **A [[Unidentified Chiss family|family]]† *Two [[Joiner]] Ruling Families **[[Catlia]] Ruling Family† **[[Massoss/Legends|Massoss]] Ruling Family† |side3= |side4= |commanders1= |commanders2= |commanders3= |commanders4= }} {{Quote|Three of the families had become critically dependent on insect labor. There was a dispute over the best course of action.''"
"''The Ascendancy had a civil war?''"
"''Chiss do not have civil wars, we have disagreements. The matter was resolved before your visit to Csilla—though I do believe you were witness to some reverberations.|[[Aristocra/Legends|Aristocra]] [[Chaf'orm'bintrano]] and [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]]|}} A '''civil conflict''' among the [[Chiss family/Legends|Chiss families]] took place at some point by [[28 ABY/Legends|28 ABY]], as a result of which the number of [[Ruling Families/Legends|Ruling Families]] in the [[Chiss Ascendancy/Legends|Chiss Ascendancy]] went from nine to four. ==The civil conflict== According to the account given by [[Aristocra/Legends|Aristocra]] [[Chaf'orm'bintrano]] in [[35 ABY/Legends|35 ABY]],''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the events of ''[[Dark Nest I: The Joiner King]]'' to [[35 ABY/Legends|35 ABY]]. the aftermath of the [[Third Vagaari War]] saw the Ruling Families of the Chiss Ascencancy recruit a labor-force from the hive-minded [[Killik/Legends|Killik]] nests. In spite of extensive precautions, two of the Families who resided on the [[Planet/Legends|planets]] [[Catlia]] and [[Massoss/Legends|Massoss]] respectively became [[Joiner]]s in the [[The Colony|Colony]], and three others became heavily dependent on Killik labor; a "disagreement" arose with the other surviving Families, who had been less compromised by the Killiks, and who allied with [[Syndic Mitth'raw'nuruodo's Household Phalanx]] to remove the three Killik-dependent lineages from power. However, the account given by Chaf'orm'bintrano is not without its problems, and may be at least partially misinformation: Formbi himself was a member of the [[Chaf family/Legends|Chaf family]], a lineage that was not among the list of four Ruling Families cited in a study produced by the [[University of Sanbra]] in the last years of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. All that can be said for sure is that the number of Ruling Families was apparently reduced substantially by [[28 ABY/Legends|28 ABY]], and at the same time, the rogue Chiss warriors of the Household Phalanx previously affiliated with the [[Empire of the Hand]] appear to have integrated themselves into the [[Chiss Expansionary Defense Force]]. ==Behind the scenes== The [[2005]] novel ''[[Dark Nest I: The Joiner King]]'' states that what occurred was not a civil war, and although the involvement of the Household Phalanx suggests that some level of military operations did take place, it is not clear whether the hostilities between the Ruling Families reached the level of a full-scale conflict. While the [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' states that of the Nine Ruling Families, four Ruling Families of the planets [[Sharb/Legends|Sharb]], [[Noris/Legends|Noris]], [[Schesa/Legends|Schesa]], and [[Ornfra/Legends|Ornfra]] fell on the losing side of this conflict and two Ruling Families of the planets Catlia and Massoss became Joiners, the 2005 novel ''Dark Nest I: The Joiner King'' states that only three Families were part of the dispute. Since it would be impossible for four Ruling Families—[[Csapla family|Csapla]], [[Nuruodo family|Nuruodo]], [[Inrokini family|Inrokini]], and [[Sabosen family|Sabosen]]—to remain standing by 28 ABY if six Ruling Families fell during this conflict instead of five,''[[The New Jedi Order: Force Heretic II: Refugee]]'' this article assumes the latter source is correct. It's unclear what happened to the [[Mitth family/Legends|Mitth family]] of [[Copero/Legends|Copero]], [[Chaf family/Legends|Chaf family]] of [[Sarvchi/Legends|Sarvchi]], and the [[Brast family]], but all of them were no longer Ruling Families. ==Appearances== *''[[Dark Nest I: The Joiner King]]'' {{1stm}} ==Sources== *''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' {{1stID|civil conflict}} *''[[The Unknown Regions]]'' {{Imo}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |es=Guerra Civil Chiss |tr=Chiss iç çatışması }} [[Category:Battles in the Chiss Ascendancy]] [[Category:Civil wars]] [[Category:Conflicts during the post-Imperial era]] [[Category:Conflicts of the Unknown Regions]]
